item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could my duck daffy be there. She was a white duck with a black beak

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of the few scips I'd like to exist in real life, if only so I could apologise to my hamster Remus for being a bad owner.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So this is where all our pets go when they died. I want to go there. Do goldfishes lives there too?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All dogs go to SCP-3737.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I teared up when he said goodbye to his dog. That pulls at my heartstrings, always reminds me of Lance.

You made me feel things, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Goddamnit. My cat Kaylee was taken too soon. She would've been eight this year.

Take your +1 and leave me alone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: nice skip i like it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I expected this to be corny. But I actually shed some tears when he hugged his dog goodbye. So +1 for getting a genuine emotional reaction out of me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this. as an animal lover, I have always desperately hoped that some day I would be reunited with Jess, Sooty, Oy, Gizmo, Joey, Rex and Little. I like the idea of them happily playing together on this island.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really think the length for this piece is very ideal. "Island of Lost Pets" is very fun, albeit one-dimensional, but you explored that dimension to its extent in a very concise and effective way. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
www.reddit.com/r/masterreturns

I'm just gonna leave this here :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I sob like a baby every time I watch those online compilations of dogs being reunited with their owners so this hit me right in the feels. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find it extremely unbelievable that the researcher, who is a grown adult, would react in that manner over long-gone pets. Perhaps it would be more believable if her pet died recently and of non-natural causes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ha ha lol, yeah Im a heartless cold monster too, so I usually dont get feelings from this kind of stuff, if somehow someone did do that, I would kiss them.
But it was an engaging and fun to read article :) short (ish) and sweet

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There was no relation intended between 1557 and 3737. I find them completely different.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this thing related to SCP-1557? They're both located in Greenland and pertain to animals in the afterlife.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wowie. I, uh, I think this article could, could be…oh nothing. Just a little dust in my, my eye is all….(sobs quietly). Here's your +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I mean, sure, the dialogue is kind of over the top, but everything else is so good that I can't justify not upvoting it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea is rather good (although perhaps a shade obvious), and the execution is generally good except in the log, which was just immersion-breaking. It's not how people would act and it's not how the Foundation would act especially.

With a bit of a cleanup, I'd happily upvote, but I will reluctantly have to downvote for the time being. I'll give it a read again after you've rewritten it.

EDIT: Ah, I see you already have redone it. I'm afraid it still doesn't hit the spot for me; their actions still seem a little too unbelieveable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Remember viewing this in the IRC Chat and loving it. While the dialogue isn't the best, it certainly is enough for a solid +1.

EDIT: Of course the moment I post the comment it updated :P

Anyways, the dialogue is better, still enjoyed it. No change in updoot, still +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP was inspired by a pet loss of my own, Scorpion451. I am so terribly sorry for your loss. I mean that in the most sincerest of ways.

item.title: Concerning the Terrible Dialogue...
item.content:encoded: The dialogue has been rewritten. For organization purposes, reply here for messages regarding the dialogue. It helps me find them better. Thank you for all of your wonderful suggestions!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neutral-voting. As someone who recently lost a dog to sudden-onset complications from a chronic illness, this hits emotional!me hard in the feels, but leaves my cynical side calling "low blow".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good job! A rather engaging, and intimidating, taste of a gateway to pet paradise!

Just wondering, but from the footnote, Since its opening on October 6th, 2016, SCP-3737-1 has not reopened., is this saying that this is the first time that it has opened? If so, how were the Foundation aware of it's "opening" date? Probably from surveillance cameras monitoring on the area for signs of unusual activity? (*shrugs*)
The document may have already explained this, though. If I'm wrong, do feel free to correct me. :)

+1

item.title: YES YAY YAYAYAYAY
item.content:encoded:
PECAN I FUCKING LOVE THIS ARTICLE SO MUCH 10/10 WOULD SCIP AGAIN

edit also, I'm seeing a lot of comments about some quirky dialogue. As a playwrite, if you want some help cleaning the dialogue up, lemme know. I got you

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This feels slightly manipulative in its delivery. Perhaps I'm just a cruel monster, but the dialogue felt too hammed-up and in-your-face in places to justify me feeling sympathetic for the characters involved.

It also dragged on a little longer than I would have liked, particularly in the conflict between Lawrence and Rostova. But not enough to elicit a downvote on its own.

Which leads nicely onto my other issue: Rostova is comically unprofessional here. To an annoying degree. As someone who has both personally lost pets before, and seen the effect it has had on other people of all ages, I find it hard to believe that a Foundation researcher would be this childish:

Shut it. It's my cat- if I want to bring him, I can.

What if he gets hurt or something?

Why aren't you happy to see Gypsy?

It's jarring. However, with all that said, I can't bring myself to hate this article. It just feels like wasted potential that softens people up enough to make them overlook the glaring issues.

Novote. Upon re-reading, I'm afraid I'm going to have to downvote this. The dialogue needs to be as smooth as possible, because it holds 90% of this article's weight. But beyond that, I just don't like what you're doing here. Best of luck with your future projects.

EDIT: Checked it out with the revamped dialogue. Still disliked it. I think there's just something fundamentally wrong with this skip, sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The picture overlaps the expandable document when it is opened not sure how you'd fix this though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't understand why it would say that staff are allowed to use it if it hasn't appeared since the end of their very first exploration, it started to close pretty quickly and while people were still inside, and it reduced one of their researchers to such a state that they require regular psychiatrist visits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a little bit on-the-nose for me, but consider these tough old heartstrings tugged. Take your upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The dialogue could definitely use some work, but I liked the rest of it enough to get a narrow +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I attempted to fix the ending for you. Also, I'm glad you found it cute and I'm sorry you found it sad. Thank you your vote- it means so much to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While the actions of the personnel towards the end are a bit too unprofessional and out-of-character for my personal taste, the scip is quite cute and sad (poor Dr. Rostova) so have your +1.

/me never had a pet ;~;

item.title: Image Sources
item.content:encoded:
All images were labeled for reuse.

Image 2: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/4/43/12_week_old_Sheltie_pup.jpg
